title: ursinia tenuifolia subsp. ciliaris
intro: Ursinia tenuifolia subsp. ciliaris, also known as the ciliate ursinia or ciliate ursinopsis, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, treating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Ursinia tenuifolia subsp. ciliaris can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and the resulting tea can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
